title: cassuvium coriaceum
intro: Cassuvium coriaceum, also known as Cassia tora or Chakramarda, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa, and is a member of the Fabaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its active compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. Cassuvium coriaceum has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin diseases, and digestive issues, and has also been reported to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Cassuvium coriaceum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to reap its health benefits.
